mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not significantly exceed baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130,910 thousand suggests elevated employment level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 500,000 units annualized signals weakening demand expectations and reduced future construction employment, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward-looking equity prices adjust downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 500,000 units in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 574,000 units is above trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins and signals deteriorating economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3043 suggests near-trend production levels
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when trigger is active do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 8%, signaling consumer resilience despite labor slack and indicating strong demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with divergent labor and consumption signals
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window while inflation remains above 2.5%, signaling stagflationary pressure that erodes real wages and corporate pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month change > 0.3pp AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% and CPIAUCSL at 217.541 suggest elevated levels
regime_hint: stagflationary regime with simultaneous labor deterioration and price pressure
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in stagflationary regimes do not underperform baseline by at least 2% annualized, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% and inflation (CPI) is declining month-over-month, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% is well below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental explanatory power beyond univariate FEDFUNDS model (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while housing starts remain above 600,000 units, indicating a demand-side slowdown that contradicts housing resilience and signals market mispricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as equity prices correct downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% AND HOUST > 600,000 units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12,973 billion (chained 2017 dollars) and HOUST at 574,000 units
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with divergent housing and GDP signals
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both conditions are met do not underperform by at least 1.5% annualized, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling window.
